name: Hiking
instructions: Begin by selecting a trail appropriate for your fitness level. Wear sturdy footwear with ankle support and dress in moisture-wicking layers.
Maintain a steady pace that allows you to hold a conversation. Use trekking poles if available for added stability and to engage your upper body.
On uphill sections, shorten your stride and lean slightly forward. On downhill sections, keep your knees slightly bent and take controlled steps to protect your joints.
Stay hydrated throughout the hike and take breaks as needed. Monitor your energy levels, especially on longer or more challenging trails.
